# Crafting Guide

Crafting turns refined ingots and gathered components into finished goods at a
crafting station. The system is built so that a crafter can never fully escape
their dependence on gatherers, because the single most important input to a good
craft is the resource quality that only careful gathering can supply.

## Recipes And Stations

Every recipe is bound to a crafting station of a matching discipline and lists
the components it consumes, the station tier it demands, and the skill rank the
crafter must have unlocked. A recipe locked behind a higher station tier simply
will not appear in the menu until the crafter has built or rented access to that
station, which keeps early crafters from skipping the progression.

## Quality Propagation

The defining rule of crafting is quality propagation: the resource quality of the
inputs carries forward into the crafted output. Feed a recipe flawless ingots and
the result inherits a flawless-leaning quality roll; feed it crude stock and the
output is crude no matter how skilled the crafter. This is why a crafter will
chase a trusted gatherer rather than buy the cheapest ore on the open market.

## Market Premium

Because quality propagates, crafters pay a steep premium for pristine and flawless
stock, and that premium is the mechanism that closes the gatherer-to-crafter
interdependence loop the whole player economy is built around. A gatherer who
consistently sources high-quality nodes can name their price, while a crafter who
skimps on inputs ships strictly worse goods and loses the downstream buyer.

## Decay And Repair

Finished goods decay with use and must be repaired at a station before their
durability reaches zero, at which point the item is destroyed permanently. Repair
consumes a fraction of the item's original components, so a flawless item is not
only better in use but cheaper to keep alive over its whole service life.
